summary.brief: Dodd v. Jones. Medical malpractice case involving emergency medicine.

evidence_index.text: Julene saw her primary care physician for breathing difficulties. A CT scan was performed which revealed a large hiatal hernia that required surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 16, 2017, Dr. Fredline performed hernia surgery on Julene at Saint Alphonsus Regional Medical Center in Nampa, Idaho.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A needle tip broke in Julene’s abdomen during the procedure; however, Dr. Fredline characterized the tip as being 'too small to pose a danger.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 19, Julene collapsed when nurses tried to help her get out of her hospital bed. Julene’s blood pressure had dropped to sixty over thirty (60/30).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The results of the CT scan revealed that there was free fluid throughout Julene’s abdomen.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Julene’s abdomen was irrigated and flushed, and the perforation was closed.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Over the next four weeks, Julene stayed at Saint Alphonsus Hospital where she suffered from acute respiratory failure, swelling so severe that her toes turned black, and her surgical wound growing larger.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On September 19, 2017, Julene was transferred to Vibra Hospital, a critical care hospital, in serious condition.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Julene could not move her limbs on her own, had a wound VAC placed on her abdomen, a tracheotomy with a speaking valve, and visible blood in her urinary catheter bag.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On November 22, 2017, Julene was transferred from Vibra Hospital to Sunny Ridge skilled nursing facility.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On February 23, 2018, Julene was discharged from Sunny Ridge to her home.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Simon opined based upon a reasonable degree of medical probability and certainty that [Saint] Alphonsus Medical Group Nampa and Dr. Forrest Fredline fell below the standard of care in the postoperative care of Julene Dodd.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Fred Simon

evidence_index.text: Dr. Simon also reaffirmed his earlier opinion that Dr. Fredline and Saint Alphonsus failed to properly respond to Julene’s worsening signs, symptoms, and complaints postoperatively.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Fred Simon

evidence_index.text: The district court judgment is affirmed.
evidence_index.speaker: Chief Justice Bevan

evidence_index.text: The district court struck the testimony of the Dodds’ experts, which was key to establishing the viability of their medical malpractice claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Chief Justice Bevan

evidence_index.text: The district court also dismissed the Dodds’ third cause of action for fraudulent concealment because Jones ultimately complied with his disclosure requirements.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Dodds’ primary argument on appeal is that Jones is judicially estopped from claiming no medical malpractice occurred as a defense to the legal malpractice claim.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: The district court exercised appropriate discretion given the legal issues presented to it.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with breathing difficulties, a CT scan revealed a large hiatal hernia.

requestables.name: CT Scan
requestables.description: CT scan of the abdomen post-collapse

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with breathing difficulties, CT scan reveals hiatal hernia.

key_events.event: Patient collapses, CT scan reveals free fluid

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient post-hernia surgery with abdominal pain
decision_points.prompt: What should be done in response to worsening abdominal pain?

options.description: Order immediate imaging and consult surgery
options.clinical_reasoning: Worsening pain could indicate a serious complication such as perforation.

options.description: Attribute pain to surgical wound and provide analgesia
options.clinical_reasoning: Pain was attributed to surgical wound without further investigation.

actual_action_defendant.description: Pain attributed to surgical wound, no immediate imaging ordered
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Pain was considered a normal postoperative symptom

expected_action_court.description: Immediate imaging and surgical consultation should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to investigate worsening symptoms led to delayed diagnosis of perforation.
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate imaging and surgical consultation could have prevented complications.

patient_outcome.description: Patient suffered from acute respiratory failure and required extended hospitalization

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to remove needle tip
Causation established - earlier intervention would have prevented complications

factual_timeline.event: CT scan reveals large hiatal hernia
Patient collapses, CT scan reveals free fluid
Hernia surgery performed, needle tip broke
Patient collapsed, CT scan revealed free fluid

treatments.outcome: Perforation closed, patient transferred to ICU

treatments.treatment: Abdomen irrigated and flushed, perforation closed

allegations.allegation: Failure to properly respond to postoperative complications

court_findings.finding: Expert testimony excluded, no breach of standard of care proven
court_findings.legal_significance: No liability found due to lack of admissible evidence
